narrative_summary:
The BRCA1 c.823G>A (p.Gly275Ser, G275S) missense change occurs in exon 10 of the BRCA1 gene, a locus implicated in hereditary breast and ovarian cancer susceptibility.  
Clinical reports are heterogeneous.  The variant is listed as pending with conflicting interpretations in the BIC database [PMC8985795] and as a variant of uncertain significance in South Asian systematic reviews and a Pakistani pancreatic‑cancer cohort [PMC8753086, PMC10640758].  By contrast, several classification efforts label it benign or likely benign: the methylation‑based and multifactorial likelihood analyses assign it Class 2 (likely non‑pathogenic) [PMC4844213] and the ENIGMA large‑scale likelihood study classifies it as likely benign [PMC6772163].  Independent databases (Lou et al., ClinVar, ENIGMA, 1000 Genomes, gnomAD) also record the variant as benign [PMC8860097, PMC10098510, PMC4513380, PMC9988940].  A single evolutionary study, however, cites a ClinVar entry that designates it pathogenic, creating a discordant annotation [PMC10993466].  
Functional evidence is limited to indirect assessments.  Methylation profiling and the multifactorial likelihood framework both support a non‑pathogenic classification for G275S [PMC4844213, PMC6772163], but no direct biochemical or cellular assays have been reported.  
Population data show the allele is rare across Asian groups (e.g., 4/307 Malay cases, 0/1385 Chinese cases, with no significant breast‑cancer association; OR = 1.49, p = 0.65) [PMC5320733] and appears at low frequency in large reference datasets (45 carriers among 4 036 individuals) [PMC10098510].  Computational predictions are discordant: PolyPhen‑2 frequently labels the change as probably damaging, whereas SIFT and other tools often predict tolerated or benign effects, and machine‑learning scores vary widely [PMC5320733, PMC8160182, PMC11442673, PMC4232638].  
Overall, the preponderance of clinical classifications, population frequency analyses, and indirect functional assessments converge on a benign or likely benign interpretation for BRCA1 G275S, despite isolated reports of pathogenicity and some in‑silico tools suggesting deleteriousness.  The current consensus therefore leans toward the variant being non‑pathogenic, though residual uncertainty remains due to conflicting database entries and limited functional validation.

Further comparison of the current classifications and the methylation derived classifications revealed very few major inconsistencies; a further 8 variants (Ala1368Val, **Gly275Ser**, Arg1347Gly, His971Arg, BRCA1 IVS 12+9 C>T, Pro346Ser, Pro727Leu, Ser475Cys) were classified by both methods as Class 1 (non pathogenic) or Class 2 (likely non pathogenic). A further three variants (Ser1655Pro, BRCA1 IVS 23+5 G>C, BRCA1 IVS 9+16 C>G) were classified by both methods in the broad uncertain Class 3, indicating for these few variants there is not enough evidence to categorize them confidently by either method. The methylation derived classification correctly predicts the Class 4 variant within this test set, BRCA1 IVS 14+2 ins 8, as Class 4. Due to missing pathology, lack of previous investigation or a combined LR between 0.5 and 2.0 (which therefore does not pass thresholds recommended by the ENIGMA BRCA classification guidelines, see http://www.enigmaconsortium.org/), six variants do not have a current classification based on multifactorial likelihood analysis or segregation analysis but are all classified by the methylation model as Class 1 or Class 2 (Ile68Arg, Leu152Phe, BRCA1 IVS 9+16C>G, Asn1236Lys, Arg1762Gly, Ser475Cys). It is therefore difficult to assess the validity of the methylation predictions for these variants. Finally, two variants with current uncertain classification (Asp214Gly and His662-Arg664del) share methylation characteristics with the less pathogenic variants of Class 1 and 2.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article reports that the BRCA1 Gly275Ser (c.823G>A) variant is classified as likely non‑pathogenic (Class 2) by both methylation‑based and conventional multifactorial analyses, indicating it is not disease‑causing.
article_mentions.title: DNA methylation profiling to assess pathogenicity of BRCA1 unclassified variants in breast cancer

mentions.quoted_text: Akter et al. showed a similar frequency in germline VUS as BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations, while Syamala et al. found all exonic or intronic sequence variations were VUS. Four unclassified variants of unknown clinical significance 942G > A (c.**823G > A**), 3238G > A (c.3119G > A), 5002T>C (c.4883T>C), and 5076G > A (c.4957G > A)] were observed in nine patients in BRCA1, and nine missense mutations of unknown clinical significance in nine patients in BRCA2, were found in a study by Juwle et al. Similarly, Darooie et al. also found four VUS, one in BRCA1 and three in BRCA2. Most of the missense mutations as VUS were described in a study by Mehta et al. , while Silva et al. found two intronic VUS (c.1910-74T>C and c.1910-51G>T) and another two VUS S775F (c.2324C>T) and P1702S (c.5104C>T)] in BRCA2. VUS are outlined in detail in Tables S1 and S2.

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The large‑scale multifactorial likelihood analysis classifies the BRCA1 G275S (c.823G>A) variant as likely benign.
article_mentions.title: Large scale multifactorial likelihood quantitative analysis of BRCA1 and BRCA2 variants: An ENIGMA resource to support clinical variant classification

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study compiles extensive Asian BRCA variant data and reports that the BRCA1 G275S (c.823G>A) variant is classified as benign in multiple databases.
article_mentions.title: Ethnic-specificity, evolution origin and deleteriousness of Asian BRCA variation revealed by over 7500 BRCA variants derived from Asian population

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study benchmarks 15 mutation‑effect prediction algorithms on a set of cancer‑related missense mutations and finds that no single tool reliably predicts pathogenicity, though combining tools modestly improves accuracy and negative predictive value.
article_mentions.title: Benchmarking mutation effect prediction algorithms using functionally validated cancer-related missense mutations

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study includes BRCA1 G275S as a benign variant in ClinVar and reports its stability predictor scores, suggesting it is unlikely pathogenic.
article_mentions.title: Evaluation of AlphaFold structure-based protein stability prediction on missense variations in cancer

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article presents PathoMAN, an automated tool for germline variant curation, and shows that for BRCA1 G275S the tool classifies it as benign while other databases report conflicting interpretations, highlighting automation benefits and classification challenges.
article_mentions.title: Towards Automation of Germline Variant Curation in Clinical Cancer Genetics

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The article introduces the GESPA tool for nsSNP pathogenicity prediction and records BRCA1 G275S as a polymorphism, indicating it is likely benign.
article_mentions.title: GESPA: classifying nsSNPs to predict disease association

article_mentions.overall_article_summary: The study lists a BRCA1 p.G275S missense variant in sarcomatoid/rhabdoid renal cell carcinoma but does not provide any functional, clinical, or pathogenicity interpretation for it.
article_mentions.title: Integrative molecular characterization of sarcomatoid and rhabdoid renal cell carcinoma
